children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: Not later than 6 months after , the Secretary shall establish a 12-member advisory committee, to be known as the Tobacco Products Scientific Advisory Committee (in this section referred to as the “Advisory Committee”).June 22, 2009

children.label: i
children.text: 7 individuals who are physicians, dentists, scientists, or health care professionals practicing in the area of oncology, pulmonology, cardiology, toxicology, pharmacology, addiction, or any other relevant specialty;

children.label: ii
children.text: 1 individual who is an officer or employee of a State or local government or of the Federal Government;

children.label: iii
children.text: 1 individual as a representative of the general public;

children.label: iv
children.text: 1 individual as a representative of the interests of the tobacco manufacturing industry;

children.label: v
children.text: 1 individual as a representative of the interests of the small business tobacco manufacturing industry, which position may be filled on a rotating, sequential basis by representatives of different small business tobacco manufacturers based on areas of expertise relevant to the topics being considered by the Advisory Committee; and

children.label: vi
children.text: 1 individual as a representative of the interests of the tobacco growers.

children.heading: Nonvoting members
children.label: B Nonvoting members
children.text: The members of the committee appointed under clauses (iv), (v), and (vi) of subparagraph (A) shall serve as consultants to those described in clauses (i) through (iii) of subparagraph (A) and shall be nonvoting representatives.

children.heading: Conflicts of interest
children.label: C Conflicts of interest
children.text: No members of the committee, other than members appointed pursuant to clauses (iv), (v), and (vi) of subparagraph (A) shall, during the member’s tenure on the committee or for the 18-month period prior to becoming such a member, receive any salary, grants, or other payments or support from any business that manufactures, distributes, markets, or sells cigarettes or other tobacco products.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: The Secretary may not appoint to the Advisory Committee any individual who is in the regular full-time employ of the Food and Drug Administration or any agency responsible for the enforcement of this chapter. The Secretary may appoint Federal officials as ex officio members.

children.heading: Chairperson
children.label: 3 Chairperson
children.text: The Secretary shall designate 1 of the members appointed under clauses (i), (ii), and (iii) of paragraph (1)(A) to serve as chairperson.
on the effects of the alteration of the nicotine yields from tobacco products;
on whether there is a threshold level below which nicotine yields do not produce dependence on the tobacco product involved; and
on its review of other safety, dependence, or health issues relating to tobacco products as requested by the Secretary.

children.heading: Compensation; support; chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: d Compensation; support; chapter 10 of title 5

children.heading: Compensation and travel
children.label: 1 Compensation and travel
children.text: Members of the Advisory Committee who are not officers or employees of the United States, while attending conferences or meetings of the committee or otherwise engaged in its business, shall be entitled to receive compensation at rates to be fixed by the Secretary, which may not exceed the daily equivalent of the rate in effect under the Senior Executive Schedule under , for each day (including travel time) they are so engaged; and while so serving away from their homes or regular places of business each member may be allowed travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, as authorized by for persons in the Government service employed intermittently.section 5382 of title 5section 5703 of title 5

children.heading: Administrative support
children.label: 2 Administrative support
children.text: The Secretary shall furnish the Advisory Committee clerical and other assistance.

children.heading: Nonapplication of chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: 3 Nonapplication of chapter 10 of title 5
children.text: does not apply to the Advisory Committee.Section 1013 of title 5

children.heading: Proceedings of advisory panels and committees
children.label: e Proceedings of advisory panels and committees
children.text: The Advisory Committee shall make and maintain a transcript of any proceeding of the panel or committee. Each such panel and committee shall delete from any transcript made under this subsection information which is exempt from disclosure under .section 552(b) of title 5
